Start Here.titleRow: India Travel Money Hacks — Top 12 Cities
headers: India Travel Money Hacks — Top 12 Cities
rows: All prices in ₹ (INR). Verified April 2026. Exchange ref: $1 ≈ ₹85, €1 ≈ ₹92, £1 ≈ ₹107
1. Daily Budget Templates — Backpacker, mid-range, luxury figures for each of the 12 cities.
2. Master Cost Sheet — Single reference table of typical prices for transport, food, monuments, lodging by city.
3. UPI & Digital Payments — How to set up UPI as a tourist (UPI One World 2026), apps to install, what to pay digitally.
4. Transport Hacks — City-by-city cheapest legit transport, official apps, fare benchmarks.
5. Overcharge Watch — Local price vs tourist price comparisons. The 'don't pay more than X' table.
6. Food & Drink Hacks — Where to eat for ₹100, ₹300, ₹1,000 in each city.
7. Shopping & Bargaining — Govt-fixed-price stores, bargain rules, when not to bargain.
8. Free & Underrated — Free attractions, free entry days, hidden free experiences.
9. City Sheets — One detailed money sheet per city (12 tabs).
10. Money Tools Glossary — Forex card vs UPI vs cash vs credit card; pros, cons, fees.
Brutally honest framing rules used throughout this workbook:
• Foreigner price ≠ tourist trap price. Most monuments have officially higher foreigner rates — that's legal and not a scam.
• UPI is the single biggest money-saver for any traveller in India in 2026 — eliminates change scams, beats forex card fees on small purchases.
• Govt fixed-price emporiums (CCIE, Khadi, state emporiums) are typically 20-40% cheaper than tourist-area shops, with no haggling needed.
• Avoid hotel currency exchange. Worst rates in the country, often 5-8% below ATM/airport rates.
• Withdraw ₹10,000-₹15,000 max per ATM trip to minimize the ₹27.14 inter-bank fee impact (RBI rule, May 2025).
• The 'foreigner price' for autos/taxis is ALWAYS app-rate × 2.5-4x. Use Uber/Ola/Rapido/Namma Yatri/GoaMiles to eliminate this.

Strong street-food culture; lower restaurant markups.

Cheaper than Delhi but Taj Mahal entry is ₹1,300 for foreigners.
Day-trippers from Delhi keep daytime prices keen.
Romantic-honeymoon market; haveli stays heavily marked up.

Heavy seasonal swing; honeymoon market spikes peak rates.
Spiritual tourism = abundance of budget guesthouses.
Use as starting benchmark; adjust by city.

Master Cost Sheet.titleRow: Master Cost Sheet — Typical Prices by City (₹)
Master Cost Sheet.subtitleRow: Verified April 2026. Use as benchmark — pay these prices, not what touts quote.

rows: UPI is THE single biggest money saver in India. Eliminates change scams, beats forex card fees on small purchases.
G20 country passport + valid Indian visa. KYC at IGI Airport / authorized partner counters (e.g. Mony app, Pyypl). Get prepaid INR wallet linked to your international mobile number.
Most foreign tourists from G20 countries. The cleanest, most legal option.
₹0 onboarding. Loading fees ~1–3% via partner. Daily limit ₹1,00,000. Wallet can hold up to ₹50,000.
Link existing NRE/NRO bank account (HDFC, ICICI, SBI, Axis) to PhonePe/GPay/Paytm with international mobile number.
Settle via Wise/Revolut/cash; friend pays for shared meals/auto rides via UPI. Use sparingly — not a scalable solution.
Cred / Paytm prepaid wallet (Indian SIM required)
Buy local SIM (₹500–₹1,500), do KYC at the SIM shop, then load via debit card.
Multi-month travelers willing to get a local SIM.
Skip the setup; rely on ATM withdrawals + Visa/MC card.
Trips under 7 days; not recommended otherwise.
ATM fees (₹27.14 + GST off-network) + 1–3% forex markup on cards.
What to Pay With What (2026 ground rules)
No tipping pressure, exact amount, GPS-tracked
Always end ride in app — no off-app payment
₹10 chai with no change hassle; vendors prefer UPI now
Vendor may say 'machine not working' to force cash overcharge — walk away
UPI = no FX markup. Card = points + fraud protection
Bill 'service charge' is NOT mandatory; you can refuse (CCPA 2022 ruling)
Fraud protection on big-ticket spends; card forex 1–3.5% beats hotel cash exchange
Hotels charging 'card fee' >2% are violating MDR caps — push back or pay UPI
Foreign debit card at HDFC/ICICI/SBI/Axis branch ATM
Lowest skimming risk; ~₹27.14 inter-bank fee per off-network withdrawal (May 2025 RBI rule)
Avoid standalone street ATMs; withdraw ₹10,000–₹15,000 max per trip
Best rates: airport SBI/Thomas Cook > FFMC kiosks > hotels (worst by 5–8%)
Always count cash before leaving counter; check FFMC license sticker
Credit card or Indian payment gateway (Razorpay/PayU)
Chargeback option if scammed; Visa/MC consumer protection
NEVER pay UPI to unknown WhatsApp 'agents' — #1 fraud in 2025–26
Tipping culture is cash-based; UPI tips feel transactional
Hotel: 10% of bill at end. Restaurant: 5–10% if no service charge
Eliminates fake-note swap. Vendors accept UPI even at flea markets now
Temples don't have UPI for offerings traditionally; some now do
₹10–₹100 per offering is fine — never feel pressured into 'minimum donation'
Cleanest UI; auto option cheaper than car
Sometimes cheaper than Uber; switch between both
Bike taxi 30–50% cheaper than auto for short rides
Zero commission to driver = fairer fare; govt-blessed
Goa has no Uber/Ola due to taxi mafia. GoaMiles is the official govt app
MUST install; foreign tourist quota (FTQ) bookable here
Real-time train info; calls also work
Compare prices; MMT often best for hotels
Best for English-language reviews from foreigners
Check menu prices BEFORE going in (anti-overcharge)
Instamart for water, snacks at standard prices
Don't ship via random shop; use known couriers
Download offline city maps before exploring
Most businesses use WhatsApp for bookings, support
Track shared costs in INR; settle via Wise
Lowest international transfer fees; free virtual debit card
Free ATM withdrawals up to limit; live FX rate
Verify exchange rates before currency exchange

Tourist autos at airport, station, monuments

Camel/horse 'palace tour' touts at Amer Fort

GoaMiles (govt app) — Uber/Ola don't work in Goa

Mumbai Metro + Local Trains (₹5–₹50). 1-day tourist pass ₹200
Local train Churchgate→Bandra ₹10 (general) / ₹100 (1st class AC); BEST bus ₹6–₹40
Black-yellow taxi without meter (rare; pickpockets in trains)
First-class AC local train pass ₹600/week beats every other transport for Mumbai
Sunrise boat ride ₹500–₹800 (shared) / ₹1,500–₹2,500 (private). Auto from station to ghats ₹100

Pre-paid airport taxi quoting fixed flat rate >₹800

Touts at Gangaur Ghat selling 'private boat'

Ferry Ernakulam→Fort Kochi ₹4 (yes, four rupees) vs auto ₹250+
The ₹4 ferry is the steal of the trip — covers most Kochi sightseeing for less than a coffee

headers: Overcharge Watch — Local Price vs Tourist Price

Tourist Trap Price (what you'll be quoted)
How to Get the Smart Price
Buy from any kirana shop or D-Mart/Reliance store; check MRP printed on bottle
Use app or official prepaid counter — never accept off-counter offers

Indian Coffee House, local kapi stalls
Govt emporiums (Khadi, state emporiums) at fixed prices

Local kirana shops or D-Mart; spices are commodity-priced
UP State Emporium or Sadar Bazar (skip the 'family workshop' tour)
Don't buy. Period. The only safe gem buy is from Bombay Bullion Association certified sellers.
Book directly via Savaari, Zoomcar, or local fleet; hotel adds 30–50% commission
Buy at City Palace ticket counter (official rate)
Walk to next ghat (Asi or Tulsi), ask 3+ boatmen, pre-fix price
Book through reputable operator (Trotters, Sahara Travels) the day before
Local salons or street artists outside tourist zones; agree price before drawing
Local ashram drop-ins; check Mysore-style schools for proper teaching
NABH-accredited centres; Kerala govt-approved 'Green Leaf' / 'Olive Leaf' rated
Local tailors in residential areas; ₹500 stitching + ₹1,000 fabric is enough
Book on BookMyShow or BCCI when sales open; never from gate touts
Buy 3 days before Holi from local shops
Bring your own phone; if you want a print, use Polaroid app + nearby printing

Food & Drink Hacks.titleRow: Food & Drink Hacks — Where to Eat for ₹100, ₹300, ₹1,000
Food & Drink Hacks.subtitleRow: Every city has cheap-eats institutions that feed locals every day at honest prices. These are them.

Gunpowder (Assagao) or Thalassa (Vagator) — ₹1,500–₹2,500 for 2 with drinks

Trishna or Mahesh Lunch Home seafood ₹1,500–₹2,500/person
Mumbai's BEST cheap eat = the dabbawala thali (₹150) — track via Zomato; or Cafe Universal at Fort

CTR Malleswaram or Vidyarthi Bhavan — ₹250–₹400 full meal

Brunton Boatyard or Old Harbour Hotel ₹1,500–₹3,000/person

Walking the Spice Route (Mattancherry → Jew Town)

Same UPI as locals use; no cash; works at 99% of merchants in cities
G20 nationals only; KYC required at landing; ₹50,000 wallet cap; merchant payments only (no P2P)
